companyNameJapanese: トヨタ自動車株式会社
description: World's largest automotive manufacturer by global vehicle sales (10.8M units FY2024), headquartered in Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture. Operates Toyota, Lexus, Daihatsu, and Hino brands. Holds ~20.6% stake in DENSO Corporation and is DENSO's primary customer (Toyota Group accounted for 55.1% of DENSO FY2025 consolidated revenue). Founded MIRISE Technologies JV with DENSO (51% DENSO / 49% Toyota) for in-vehicle semiconductor R&D. Co-investor in JASM (TSMC Kumamoto fab) to secure domestic semiconductor supply for Toyota/DENSO vehicles. Key vehicle platforms include the bZ electrification series (BEV), Prius/RAV4 HEV/PHEV, and Lexus EV. Also holds significant stakes in Aisin (7259.T), Subaru (7270.T), Suzuki (7269.T), and Mazda (7261.T).

_externalRelationships.densoRelationship: Toyota is DENSO's parent company (~20.6% stake) and primary customer. Toyota Group (Toyota Motor + Toyota Industries + Toyota Fudosan + Daihatsu + Hino + Aisin) accounted for 55.1% of DENSO FY2025 total consolidated sales. Co-founder of MIRISE Technologies semiconductor R&D JV (49% Toyota). Co-investor in JASM (TSMC Kumamoto fab, ~2% stake). Toyota's next-gen bZ EV platform relies heavily on DENSO's SiC inverters, onboard chargers, and thermal management systems.
keySuppliers: DENSO (6902.T, CMP-0122) — powertrain, thermal, electrification, ADAS electronics
Aisin (7259.T, CMP-0125) — transmissions, chassis, body systems
Panasonic (6752.T) — EV batteries (Prime Planet Energy & Solutions JV)

_metadata.dataSource: DENSO CMP-0122 deep research (Toyota Group 55.1% of DENSO revenue, 20.6% shareholder), Toyota Motor IR, annual reports
_metadata.notes: Added as DENSO (CMP-0122) primary client and parent company. Market cap approximate as of Feb 2026 (~32T JPY). Financial details pending dedicated deep research.
